---
format:
  html:
    footnotes-hover: false
    css: styles.css
    toc: true
    toc-title: On this page
    toc-location: left
mainfont: "GT-America"
linkcolor: "#00355a"
anchor-sections: false
params:
  state: "WA"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<head>
<img src='https://csg-state-violent-crime.netlify.app/img/csgjc-logo.png' style='display: none;'> 
</head>

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(csgjcr)
library(highcharter)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(reactable)
library(reactablefmtr)

 
source("R/utils.R")

# set path to data folder on sharepoint
sp_data_to_plot_path <- csg_sp_path("ad_hoc_requests/state_violent_crime_marshall/data")

### set project path for website update analytic file folder
sp_data_site_updates_path <- csg_sp_path("ad_hoc_requests/state_violent_crime_marshall/data/website_updates")

state <- csg_state_convert(params$state, from = "abbr", "name")
state_abbr_params <- params$state
state_name_text <- if_else(params$state == "DC", "the District of Columbia", state)

# read in crime data
srs_state <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_state.rds")) |> 
  filter(state_name == state, crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2013) |>
  mutate(
    incidents_solved = incidents_reported - incidents_unsolved,
    pct_solved = incidents_solved / incidents_reported
  )

srs_by_cat_state <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_by_cat_state.rds")) |> 
  filter(state_name == state, crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2013) |>
  mutate(
    incidents_solved = incidents_reported - incidents_unsolved,
    pct_solved = incidents_solved / incidents_reported
  )


srs_state_all <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_state.rds")) |> 
    filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2013)

srs_by_cat_us <- srs_state_all |>
  group_by(year, crime_cat, state_name = "United States", state_abbr = "U.S.") |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved),
    \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total / 4,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    # pct_unsolved = incidents_unsolved / incidents_reported,
    incidents_solved = incidents_reported - incidents_unsolved,
    pct_solved = incidents_solved / incidents_reported
  )

srs_us <- srs_state_all |>
  group_by(year, state_name = "United States", state_abbr = "U.S.", group) |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved), sum, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    # pct_unsolved = incidents_unsolved / incidents_reported,
    incidents_solved = incidents_reported - incidents_unsolved,
    pct_solved = incidents_solved / incidents_reported
  )

srs_solved_by_county_to_map <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, 
                                             "viol_crime_unsolved_by_county_to_map_clean.rds")) |>
  filter(acs_county_state_name == state) |> 
  mutate(n_solved_2023 = n_reported_2023 - n_unsolved_2023,
         solved_rate_2023 = n_solved_2023 / n_reported_2023)

srs_solved_by_agency_to_table <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, 
                                             "viol_crime_unsolved_by_agency_to_table.rds")) |>  
  filter(state_name == state) 

### create object with states to exclude from solve rate analysis
states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz <- c("Illinois")

### create object with states to exclude from UCR officer surveys due to missingness
states_to_exclude_from_ucr_officer_count_analysis <- c("Nebraska")

# create title of page
# we don't have IL clearance rate data so take that out of title
doc_title <- case_when(
  state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz ~ paste(state, "data on violent crime"),
  state == "District of Columbia" ~ paste(state, "data on violent crime and arrests"),
  TRUE ~ paste(state, "data on violent crime and arrests"),
  )

### create dynamic text for 3 states with low reporting numbers
low_reporting_dynamic <- case_when(
  state == "Florida" ~ paste0("Note: Florida's 2023 estimates were based on data from 47 percent fewer agencies and covered 35 percent less of the population compared to 2020."),
  state == "Mississippi" ~ paste0("Note: Mississippi's 2023 estimates were based on data submitted from 15 percent more agencies but covered 16 percent less of the population compared to 2020."),
    state == "Wyoming" ~ paste0("Note: Wyoming's 2023 estimates were based on data submitted by 10 percent fewer agencies and covered 10 percent less of the population compared to 2020."),
  TRUE ~ paste0(""),
  )

axis_expansion_mult <- 0.5
```

```{r }
### import df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_agency_and_type_to_table_2023 for second table
df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_agency_and_type_to_table_2023 <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"website_update_viol_crime_unsolved_by_agency_and_type.rds"))

### import asslgf_clean for lea expenditure analysis
asslgf_clean <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"website_update_state_level_police_expenditure_inflation_adjusted.rds")) |> 
  mutate(state_abbr = toupper(state_abbr)) |> 
  filter(year>=2013,
         state_abbr==state_abbr_params) 

### import ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean for officer workload analysis
ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"website_update_lea_staffing_workload_by_state.rds")) |> 
  mutate(state_abbr = toupper(state_abbr)) |> 
  filter(year>=2013,
         state_abbr==state_abbr_params) 

### import ucr_employee_data_national_clean for officer workload analysis -- US-wide
ucr_employee_data_national_clean <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"website_update_lea_staffing_workload_national.rds")) |> 
  filter(year>=2013) |>
  mutate(state_name = "United States",
         state_abbr = "U.S.")

```

```{r }
### import csg region information 
### to include bar for region's data alongside state's data in supplemental homicide section
csg_regions_filtered <- read_csv(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path,"csg-regions.csv")) |>
  mutate(state_abbr = csgjcr::csg_state_convert(state_name,
                                                "name",
                                                "abbr"),
         region_to_keep = paste0(csg_region[state_abbr==state_abbr_params])) |> 
  ### keep only states in same region as state we're analyzing
  ### drop state for current page -- we want all states in region aside from this state
  filter(region_to_keep==csg_region,
         state_abbr!=state_abbr_params) |> 
  dplyr::select(-region_to_keep) |> 
  mutate(csg_region = paste0("Other ",csg_region," States"))

### create label for later dynamic text
csg_regions_raw <- read_csv(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path,"csg-regions.csv")) |>
  mutate(state_abbr = csgjcr::csg_state_convert(state_name,
                                                "name",
                                                "abbr"),
         region_to_keep = paste0(csg_region[state_abbr==state_abbr_params])) |> 
  ### keep only states in same region as state we're analyzing
  ### drop state for current page -- we want all states in region aside from this state
  filter(region_to_keep==csg_region,
         state_abbr!=state_abbr_params) |> 
  dplyr::select(-region_to_keep)

csg_region <- paste0(csg_regions_raw$csg_region[1])

csg_region_full <- paste0("CSG ",csg_region," Region")

```


```{r }
### now import all supplemental homicide dfs for visualization
### these files were cleaned and created here: R/12-prep-files-for-website-updates-2024.qmd

### shr_1976_2022_state_overall
shr_1976_2022_state_overall <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2022_state_overall_clear.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2022_state_relationship
shr_1976_2022_state_relationship <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2022_state_victim_offender_relationship.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2022_state_single_multiple_victims
shr_1976_2022_state_single_multiple_victims <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2022_state_single_multiple_victims.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2022_state_weapon
shr_1976_2022_state_weapon <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2022_state_weapon.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2022_state_circumstance
shr_1976_2022_state_circumstance <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2022_state_incident_circumstance.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2022_state_race
shr_1976_2022_state_race <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2022_state_victim_race_eth.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2022_state_gender
shr_1976_2022_state_gender <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2022_state_victim_gender.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2022_state_race_sex
shr_1976_2022_state_race_sex <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2022_state_victim_race_gender.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2022_state_age
shr_1976_2022_state_age <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2022_state_victim_age.rds")) 

```

---
title: "`r doc_title`"
---

```{r}
min_max_viol_crime <- srs_by_cat_state |> 
  filter(year %in% range(year)) |> 
  arrange(year)

max_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[2]
min_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[1] 

pct_change_crime <- (min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[2] - min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[1]) / min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[1]

change_text_crime <- case_when(
  pct_change_crime > 0.005 &
    pct_change_crime < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_crime * 100), 1),
                                    " percent higher than"),
  pct_change_crime >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_crime * 100), 1),
                                    " percent higher than"),
  pct_change_crime < -0.005 &
    pct_change_crime > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_crime * 100), 1),
                                    " percent lower than"),
  pct_change_crime <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_crime * 100), 1),
                                    " percent lower than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
  )

us_state_rate_comp <- (min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[2] - srs_by_cat_us[srs_by_cat_us$year==2023, ]$incidents_reported_rate_total) / srs_by_cat_us[srs_by_cat_us$year==2023, ]$incidents_reported_rate_total

us_state_rate_text_crime <- case_when(
  us_state_rate_comp > 0.005 &
    us_state_rate_comp < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(us_state_rate_comp * 100), 1),
                                    " percent higher than"),
  us_state_rate_comp >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(us_state_rate_comp * 100), 1),
                                    " percent higher than"),
  us_state_rate_comp < -0.005 &
    us_state_rate_comp > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(us_state_rate_comp * 100), 1),
                                    " percent lower than"),
  us_state_rate_comp <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(us_state_rate_comp * 100), 1),
                                    " percent lower than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
  )


pct_change_solved <- min_max_viol_crime$pct_solved[2] - min_max_viol_crime$pct_solved[1]

change_text_clear <- case_when(
  pct_change_solved > 0.005 &
    pct_change_solved < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_solved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage point higher than"),
  pct_change_solved >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_solved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage points higher than"),
  pct_change_solved < -0.005 &
    pct_change_solved > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_solved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage point lower than"),
  pct_change_solved <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_solved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage points lower than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
  )
```

## Violent crime in `r state_name_text`

```{r}
crime_rate_max <- paste0(comma(min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[2], 1, scale = 1e5))
```

In `r max_year`, `r crime_rate_max` violent crime incidents per 100,000 residents were reported to police in `r state_name_text`. This is `r change_text_crime` the violent crime rate in `r min_year`.

The crime rate in `r state_name_text` in 2023 was `r us_state_rate_text_crime` the United States average.

Data regarding crime and solve rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr). Violent crime includes homicide, aggravated assault, robbery, and rape. Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total population for a given geography. For more information, see the data sources and methodology section below.

`r low_reporting_dynamic`

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_by_cat_state |> 
  bind_rows(srs_by_cat_us) |> 
  mutate(
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1)
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = jr_pal[c(1, 7)],
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()

```

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_state |> 
  bind_rows(srs_us) |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  group_by(year, state_abbr) |>
  mutate(
    tot_crime = sum(incidents_reported),
    pct = incidents_reported / tot_crime,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5
    ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    group = fct_reorder(group, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Incidents reported per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Total violent incidents reported: ", comma(tot_crime, 1), "<br>",
      "Share of all violent incidents: ", percent(pct, 1)
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    ) |> 
  arrange(year, group)
```

## Homicide

```{r}
to_plot_2 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Homicide")

rng <- range(to_plot_2$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_2 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = c(unique(to_plot_2$color), jr_pal[7]),
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Homicide incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |>
  hc_setup()

```

## Aggravated assault

```{r}
to_plot_3 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Aggravated assault")

rng <- range(to_plot_3$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_3 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = c(unique(to_plot_3$color), jr_pal[7]),
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Aggravated assault in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where aggravated assault was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

## Robbery

```{r}
to_plot_4 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Robbery")

rng <- range(to_plot_4$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_4 |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = c(unique(to_plot_4$color), jr_pal[7]),
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Robbery in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where robbery was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |>   hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

## Rape

```{r}
to_plot_5 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Rape")

rng <- range(to_plot_5$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_5 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = c(unique(to_plot_5$color), jr_pal[7]),
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),    
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Rape in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where rape was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot |>
  filter(state_name == state) |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    stacking = "normal",
    groupPadding = 0.05,
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text, ", by offense")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |> 
  hc_legend(reversed = "true") |>
  hc_setup()
```

:::

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("## Solved violent crime in", state_name_text)
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz}

### create state-specific labels for text output
solved_rate_max <- paste0(comma(min_max_viol_crime$pct_solved[2] * 100, 1), " percent")

state_average_agg_assault_solve_rate <- paste0(scales::comma(srs_state$pct_solved[srs_state$year==max(srs_state$year) & srs_state$group=="Aggravated assault"]*100,1))
  

state_average_homicide_solve_rate <- paste0(scales::comma(srs_state$pct_solved[srs_state$year==max(srs_state$year) & srs_state$group=="Homicide"]*100,1))

state_average_rape_solve_rate <- paste0(scales::comma(srs_state$pct_solved[srs_state$year==max(srs_state$year) & srs_state$group=="Rape"]*100,1))


```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat(paste0(
  "In ", max_year, ", ", solved_rate_max, " of violent crimes reported to police in ", state_name_text, " were solved. This is ", change_text_clear, " the solve rate in ", min_year, ".", '\n','\n',
    
    "Solve rates are the number of reported crimes cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. For more information, see the data sources and methodology section below."
))
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("
::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime
")
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz}
to_plot <- srs_by_cat_state |> 
  bind_rows(srs_by_cat_us) |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  mutate(
    ### shouldn't be an issue for overall viz
    ### but just in case, recoding any solved rate below 0 to 0
    pct_solved_clean = ifelse(pct_solved<0 & !is.na(pct_solved),
                          0,pct_solved),
    pct_solved_clean = pct_solved_clean * 100,
    ### create pct_solved and incidents_solved for tooltip
    ### where if value is below 0, explanatory text is displayed
    ### instead of number
    pct_solved_tooltip = ifelse(pct_solved<0 & !is.na(pct_solved),
                                  "Less than 0%",as.character(percent(pct_solved_clean / 100, 1))),
    incidents_solved_tooltip = ifelse(incidents_solved<0 & !is.na(incidents_solved),
                                  "Fewer than 0",as.character(comma(incidents_solved))),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Solve rate: ", pct_solved_tooltip, "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents solved: ", incidents_solved_tooltip
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$pct_solved_clean)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult
y_max <- if_else(y_max > 100, 100, y_max)

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_solved_clean, group = state_name),
    color = jr_pal[c(1, 7)],
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, solve rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste("Violent crime solve rate in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program<br>Aggregated from SRS agency-level Offenses Known reports") |> 
  # hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()

```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("
## By offense
")
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz}

to_plot <- srs_state |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  mutate(
    ### recode any solved rate below 0 to 0
    pct_solved_clean = ifelse(pct_solved<0 & !is.na(pct_solved),
                          0,pct_solved),
    pct_solved_clean = pct_solved_clean * 100,
    ### create pct_solved and incidents_solved for tooltip
    ### where if value is below 0, explanatory text is displayed
    ### instead of number
    pct_solved_tooltip = ifelse(pct_solved<0 & !is.na(pct_solved),
                                  "Less than 0%",as.character(percent(pct_solved_clean / 100, 1))),
    incidents_solved_tooltip = ifelse(incidents_solved<0 & !is.na(incidents_solved),
                                  "Fewer than 0",as.character(comma(round(incidents_solved,
                                                                          digits=0))))) |> 
  mutate(
    group = fct_reorder(group, pct_solved, last),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Solve rate: ", pct_solved_tooltip, "<br>",
      "Incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Incidents solved: ", incidents_solved_tooltip
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(desc(year), pct_solved_clean)

# rng <- range(to_plot$pct_solved_clean)
# mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult
# 
# y_min <- rng[1] - mult
# y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
# y_max <- rng[2] + mult
# y_max <- if_else(y_max > 100, 100, y_max)

to_plot |>   
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_solved_clean, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, solve rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )       
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime solve rate in ", state_name_text, ", by offense")) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program<br>Aggregated from SRS agency-level Offenses Known reports") |> 
  # hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("
:::
")
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("## Solved violent crime by county in", state_name_text)
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz}
total_agencies <- comma(n_distinct(srs_solved_by_agency_to_table$ori))

counties_represented <- comma(n_distinct(srs_solved_by_agency_to_table$county_clean))
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### choose blues from csgjcr for now
blue1 <- "#d4e9f8"
blue2 <- "#9ed4ef"
blue4 <- "#007392"
blue5 <- "#00475d"

### add tooltip
srs_solved_by_county_to_map_tooltip <- srs_solved_by_county_to_map |> 
  ### removing counties' solved rate if they have fewer than 25 incidents reported in 2022
  ### we want them grayed out on map
  mutate(solved_rate_2023 = ifelse(n_reported_2023<25,
                                     NA,solved_rate_2023)) |> 
  ### recode any solved counts or rates less than 0 to 0
  mutate(solved_rate_2023 = ifelse(solved_rate_2023<0 & !is.na(solved_rate_2023),
                                     0,solved_rate_2023),
         n_solved_2023 = ifelse(n_solved_2023<0 & !is.na(n_solved_2023),
                                     0,n_solved_2023)) |>
  ### convert solve rate from decimal to whole number -- for map legend
  mutate(solved_rate_2023 = solved_rate_2023*100) |> 
  # ### recode NaN and Inf values as NA
  # mutate(across(.cols = solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022, 
  #               ~ ifelse(is.infinite(.x) | is.nan(.x), NA, .x))) |> 
  # ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  # ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(tooltip = case_when(
    n_reported_2023>=25 & !is.na(n_reported_2023) ~ paste0(
    "<b>", county_clean, " – 2023", "</b><br>",
      "Solve rate: ", comma(solved_rate_2023, 1),"%", "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(n_reported_2023, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents solved: ", comma(n_solved_2023, 1), "<br>",
      "Agencies reporting data: ", comma(n_agencies, 1), "<br>",
      "Population covered by reporting agencies: ", comma(nibrs_reported_pop_coverage_pct,1), "%", "<br>"
      ),
    n_reported_2023<25 & !is.na(n_reported_2023) ~ paste0(
      "Agencies in ", county_clean, " did not report enough", "<br>",
      "data on solved crime to the FBI to", "<br>",
      "make reliable estimates (fewer than 25 incidents)."
      ),
    is.na(n_reported_2023)==TRUE ~ paste0(
      "No agencies in ", county_clean, " reported data", "<br>",
      "on solved crime to the FBI."
      )
    )
    ) 

### extract state abbr for hc map pull
state_abbr <- tolower(head(srs_solved_by_county_to_map_tooltip$state_abbr[!is.na(srs_solved_by_county_to_map_tooltip$state_abbr)],1))


### pull only state geo that we want for each param iteration
hcmap(
  paste0("countries/us/us-",state_abbr,"-all"),
  data = srs_solved_by_county_to_map_tooltip,
  value = "solved_rate_2023",
  joinBy = c("fips","county_fips"),
  nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
  nullInteraction = TRUE,
  borderWidth = 0.45,
  borderColor = "black",
  accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "county: {point.acs_county_name}, solve rate: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )) |> 
  hc_title(
    text = "Violent crime solve rate, 2023",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = header_font, color = "#005FAD",
                              fontSize = "24px", fontWeight = "bold")
    ) |>   
  hc_colorAxis(endOnTick = TRUE,
               startOnTick = TRUE,
               stops = color_stops(4, c(blue1,
                                        blue2,
                                        blue4,
                                        blue5)),
               labels = list(format = "{value}%",
                             style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                                          fontFamily = default_fonts))) |> 
  hc_legend(align = "center",
            horizontalAlign = "bottom",
            layout = "horizontal",
            # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
            symbolHeight = 25,
            symbolWidth = 315,
            x = 0,
            y = 0
            ) |> 
     hc_tooltip(
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
        formatter = JS("function() {
        return '<div style=\"background-color: #FFFFFF; opacity: 1; padding: 8px; border: none;\">' +
        this.point.tooltip +
        '</div>';}"),
        useHTML = TRUE
      ) |>
  hc_setup()

```

<br>

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}

cat("## Solved violent crime by agency in", state_name_text)
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}

cat(paste0(
  "In the following table, violent crime incident rates, as well as violent crime solve rates, are included for each of the ", total_agencies, " law enforcement agencies that reported data in ",state_name_text," in 2023. Violent crime solve rates by offense differ across agencies across the state. The average solve rate for aggravated assault is ",state_average_agg_assault_solve_rate ," percent. Homicide is ",state_average_homicide_solve_rate," percent, and rape is ",state_average_rape_solve_rate, " percent.  
  

Please note that rates calculated for agencies with smaller jurisdiction populations, as well as fewer overall incidents, are less reliable than those for larger jurisdictions and should be interpreted with caution."))
```


::: {.panel-tabset}


```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("### Overall rates")
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz}

srs_solved_by_agency_to_table |> 
  ### overwrite rate  with NA if agency is missing population (current value is .inf)
  mutate(n_reported_rate = ifelse(population==0,
                                  NA,n_reported_rate)) |>
  ### remove state name and other additional columns
  ### we just needed it to filter on param
  select(agency_name,
         county_clean,
         population,
         n_reported_rate,
         n_reported,
         solved_rate,
         solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023) |> 
  ### round solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023 for successful use 
  ### of add_plus_sign_percent_point_change function
  mutate(solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023 = as.numeric(round(solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023,
                                                            digits = 0))) |>
  arrange(desc(population)) |>
  reactable_template(
    sort_col = list("population" = "desc"),
    columns = list(
      agency_name = colDef(name = "Agency", minWidth = 250,
                             sticky = "left",
      # Add a right border style to visually distinguish the sticky column
      style = list(borderRight = "1px solid #eee"),
      headerStyle = list(borderRight = "1px solid #eee")),
      county_clean = colDef(name = "County", minWidth = 108),
      population = colDef(name = "Jurisdiction Population", minWidth = 130), 
      n_reported = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents Reported (2023)"), 
      n_reported_rate = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents per 100k (2023)",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE)), 
      solved_rate = colDef(
        name = "Solve Rate (2023)",
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE)
        ),
      solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2023 = colDef(
        name = "Percentage point change in solve rate (2019 to 2023)",
        cell = add_plus_sign_percent_point_change
        )
      )
    )

```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("### By offense")
```


<span style="color:#E17619"> Orange </span> indicates a solve rate less than the state average.

<span style="color:#15607A"> Blue </span> indicates a solve rate greater than or equal to the state average.


```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz}

#############################################
### prep agency-level df for table
#############################################

### clean up rates and select columns we need
df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_agency_and_type_to_table_2023_final <-
  df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_agency_and_type_to_table_2023 |> 
  filter(state_abbr==state_abbr_params,
         jurisdiction_population>0) |> 
  ### overwrite rate  with NA if agency is missing population (current value is .inf)
  mutate(n_reported_rate_aggravated_assault = ifelse(jurisdiction_population==0,
                                  NA,n_reported_rate_aggravated_assault),
         n_reported_rate_homicide = ifelse(jurisdiction_population==0,
                                  NA,n_reported_rate_homicide),
         n_reported_rate_rape = ifelse(jurisdiction_population==0,
                                  NA,n_reported_rate_rape),
         n_reported_rate_robbery = ifelse(jurisdiction_population==0,
                                  NA,n_reported_rate_robbery)) |>
  ### remove state name and other additional columns
  ### we just needed it to filter on param
  select(agency_name, 
         # agency_type, 
         # state_abbr,
         county, 
         jurisdiction_population, 
         n_reported_rate_aggravated_assault,
         solved_rate_aggravated_assault, 
         n_reported_rate_homicide, 
         solved_rate_homicide, 
         n_reported_rate_rape, 
         solved_rate_rape, 
         n_reported_rate_robbery, 
         solved_rate_robbery) 

### determine median solve rates by violent offense type for state
### we'll use these state averages to develop an agency-level comparison in the table
state_median_solved_rate_aggravated_assault <- median(df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_agency_and_type_to_table_2023_final$solved_rate_aggravated_assault,
                                                      na.rm=TRUE)

state_median_solved_rate_homicide <- median(df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_agency_and_type_to_table_2023_final$solved_rate_homicide,
                                                      na.rm=TRUE)

state_median_solved_rate_rape <- median(df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_agency_and_type_to_table_2023_final$solved_rate_rape,
                                                      na.rm=TRUE)

state_median_solved_rate_robbery <- median(df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_agency_and_type_to_table_2023_final$solved_rate_robbery,
                                                      na.rm=TRUE)


### final prep df for table
df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_agency_and_type_to_table_2023_final |> 
  mutate(across(c(solved_rate_aggravated_assault,
                  solved_rate_homicide,
                  solved_rate_rape,
                  solved_rate_robbery
                  ), 
                ~ replace(.x, is.nan(.x), NA))
         ) |> 
  reactable_template(
    sort_col = list("jurisdiction_population" = "desc"),
    columns = list(
      agency_name = colDef(name = "Agency", minWidth = 250,
                             sticky = "left",
      # Add a right border style to visually distinguish the sticky column
      style = list(borderRight = "1px solid #eee"),
      headerStyle = list(borderRight = "1px solid #eee")),
      county = colDef(name = "County", minWidth = 108),
      jurisdiction_population = colDef(name = "Jurisdiction Population", minWidth = 150), 
      n_reported_rate_aggravated_assault = colDef(name = "Aggravated Assault Incidents per 100k",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE), minWidth = 120), 
      solved_rate_aggravated_assault = colDef(
        name = paste0("Aggravated Assault Solve Rate",'<br>','<br>', "(",state_abbr," Agency Average: ",scales::percent(state_median_solved_rate_aggravated_assault,accuracy=1),")"),
        html = TRUE,
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE), 
        minWidth = 120,
        style = function(value) {
      if (value >= state_median_solved_rate_aggravated_assault & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#15607A"
      } else if (value <= state_median_solved_rate_aggravated_assault & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#E17619"
      } else {
        color <- "black"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    }
        
        ),
      n_reported_rate_homicide = colDef(name = "Homicide Incidents per 100k",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE), 
                               align = "center",
          ),
      solved_rate_homicide = colDef(
        name = paste0("Homicide Solve Rate",'<br>','<br>',"(",state_abbr," Agency Average: ",scales::percent(state_median_solved_rate_homicide,accuracy=1),")"),
        html = TRUE,
        minWidth = 120,
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE),
        align = "center",
       style = function(value) {
      if (value >= state_median_solved_rate_homicide & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#15607A"
      } else if (value <= state_median_solved_rate_homicide & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#E17619"
      } else {
        color <- "black"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    }),

      n_reported_rate_rape = colDef(name = "Rape Incidents per 100k",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE)),
      solved_rate_rape = colDef(
        name = paste0("Rape Solve Rate",'<br>','<br>',"(",state_abbr," Agency Average: ",scales::percent(state_median_solved_rate_rape,accuracy=1),")"),
        html = TRUE,
        minWidth = 155,
        align = "left",
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE),
        style = function(value) {
      if (value >= state_median_solved_rate_rape & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#15607A"
      } else if (value <= state_median_solved_rate_rape & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#E17619"
      } else {
        color <- "black"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    }),
      n_reported_rate_robbery = colDef(name = "Robbery Incidents per 100k",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE)), 
      solved_rate_robbery = colDef(
        name = paste0("Robbery Solve Rate",'<br>','<br>',"(",state_abbr," Agency Average: ",scales::percent(state_median_solved_rate_robbery,accuracy=1),")"),
        html = TRUE,  
        minWidth = 120,
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE),
                style = function(value) {
      if (value >= state_median_solved_rate_robbery & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#15607A"
      } else if (value <= state_median_solved_rate_robbery & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#E17619"
      } else {
        color <- "black"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    })
    ))



```

:::


<br>

```{r, results='asis'}
cat("## Law enforcement resources in", state_name_text)
```

```{r}
### create dynamic labels for text blurb below
asslgf_clean_percent_change <- asslgf_clean |> 
  filter(year %in% range(year)) |> 
  arrange(year)

max_year_resources <- max(asslgf_clean_percent_change$year)
min_year_resources <- min(asslgf_clean_percent_change$year)

pct_change_spending <- scales::percent(((asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources] - asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources]) / asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources]),
                                       accuracy = 1)

pct_change_spending_abs_value <- scales::comma(abs((asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources] - asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources]) / asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources])*100,
                                       accuracy = 1)


change_spending_text_clear <- case_when(
  pct_change_spending >= 0 ~ paste0("increased"),
  pct_change_spending < 0 ~ paste0("decreased")
  # TRUE ~ " changed"
  )


```

```{r}

### clean up ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean for text output below
ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change <- ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean |>
  filter(year %in% range(year)) |>
  arrange(year)

```


```{r}

### code min and max years for labels below
max_year_resources_staff_per_offense <- max(ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year)
min_year_resources_staff_per_offense <- min(ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year)

### now create pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense and change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear
### for text below

pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense <- scales::percent((ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources_staff_per_offense] - ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources_staff_per_offense]) / ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources_staff_per_offense],
                                       accuracy = 1)


pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense_abs_value <- scales::comma(abs((ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources_staff_per_offense] - ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources_staff_per_offense]) / ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources_staff_per_offense])*100,
                                       accuracy = 1)

change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear <- case_when(
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense >= 0 ~ paste0("increased"),
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense < 0 ~ paste0("decreased")
  # TRUE ~ " has stayed nearly the same"
  )


change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear_1 <- case_when(
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense >= 0 ~ paste0("increase"),
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense < 0 ~ paste0("decrease")
  # TRUE ~ " has stayed nearly the same"
  )

change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear_2 <- case_when(
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense >= 0 ~ paste0("an increase"),
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense < 0 ~ paste0("a decrease")
  # TRUE ~ " has stayed nearly the same"
  )

### now code opposite for discussion below
change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear_3 <- case_when(
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense >= 0 ~ paste0("a decrease"),
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense < 0 ~ paste0("an increase")
  # TRUE ~ " has stayed nearly the same"
  )

```

```{r, results='asis'}

cat(paste0(
  state_name_text," ", change_spending_text_clear, " spending on law enforcement by ", pct_change_spending_abs_value ," percent from 2013 to 2021, adjusting for inflation. Expenditures include local, state, and federal spending on local police departments, sheriffs’ offices, and state police agencies such as highway patrols and other state investigative law enforcement agencies.  

Law enforcement resources also refer to the state’s staffing per 10,000 residents and per 1,000 violent crimes. These factors may show the level of policing a community receives, potentially influencing both crime and solve rates."))
```


```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_ucr_officer_count_analysis, results='asis'}
cat(paste0(
"Additionally, the number of officers per 1k violent crimes in ",state_name_text," has ",change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear," by ",pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense_abs_value, " percent from 2013 to 2023. This ",change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear_1," may be due to a combination of ",change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear_2," in law enforcement officers and/or ",change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear_3," in reported violent crimes. 

Law enforcement staffing can impact how agencies allocate resources to solve violent crime. For example, severe staff shortages can reduce investigative personnel or increase the number of cases that individual officers are managing."
))
```

::: {.panel-tabset}



<br>

```{r, results='asis'}
cat("### Expenditures")
```


```{r }
to_plot <- asslgf_clean |> 
  mutate(
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Inflation-adjusted expenditure: ", scales::dollar(n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars, 
                                                        accuracy = 1,
                                                        prefix = '$')))

rng <- range(to_plot$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Law enforcement expenditures in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Inflation-adjusted law enforcement expenditures, 2013–2021") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "US Census Bureau Annual Survey of State and Local Government Finances") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    title = "",
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    labels = list(
      formatter = JS("function() { return '$' + (this.value / 1000000000) + 'B'; }")
    )) |> 
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |>
  hc_setup()

```

<br>

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_ucr_officer_count_analysis, results='asis'}
cat("### Officers per capita")
```


```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_ucr_officer_count_analysis}

to_plot <- ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean |> 
  bind_rows(ucr_employee_data_national_clean) |> 
  mutate(
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Officers per 10k residents: ", scales::comma(officer_pop_rate_per_10k, 
                                                        accuracy = .1)),
        state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$officer_pop_rate_per_10k)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult *2.5

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, officer_pop_rate_per_10k,
          group = state_name),
    color = jr_pal[c(1, 7)],
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Officers per capita in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Officers per 10k residents, 2013–2023") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates<br>FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program Police Employee Data") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |> 
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
    hc_yAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}")
    ) |>
  hc_setup()

```

<br>

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_ucr_officer_count_analysis, results='asis'}
cat("### Officers per violent offense")
```


```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_ucr_officer_count_analysis}

to_plot <- ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean |> 
  bind_rows(ucr_employee_data_national_clean) |> 
  mutate(
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Officers per 1k violent crimes: ", scales::comma(officer_per_violent_offense_1k, 
                                                        accuracy = .1)),
        state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$officer_per_violent_offense_1k)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult


to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, officer_per_violent_offense_1k,
          group = state_name),
    color = jr_pal[c(1, 7)],
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Officers per violent offense in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Officers per 1k violent crimes, 2013–2023") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates<br>FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program Police Employee Data") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |> 
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
    hc_yAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}")
    ) |>
  hc_setup()


```

:::

<br>

```{r, results='asis' }
cat("## Exploration of homicide solve rates in", state_name_text)
```


Explore homicide solve rates by available victim demographics and case characteristics for `r state_name_text` and other states in the [`r csg_region_full`](https://www.csg.org/regional-offices). Grouping states by region allows policymakers to compare their state to others that may be similar. Solve rates are included by race and ethnicity, gender, age, weapon type, and number of victims. For more information, see the data sources and methodology below.


::: {.panel-tabset}


### Race and ethnicity 

```{r }

### clean up df for plot via function
### this function will prepare a df for plotting -- grouping by
### varying incident or demographic characteristics
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_plot(df = shr_1976_2022_state_race,
                                          var = "victim_1_race_clean") |> 
  ### arrange legend so that state name comes first followed by region name
  mutate(group_for_plot = fct_relevel(group_for_plot, paste0("Other ",csg_region," States"), after = Inf))


### now plot
to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(victim_1_race_clean, 
          clearance_rate,
          group = group_for_plot),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by race and ethnicity")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2018–2022") |>
  hc_caption(text = list("This analysis excludes records where a victim's race and ethnicity is unknown<br>FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data")) |>     # hc_add_theme(hc_theme_jc) |>
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()

```



### Gender

```{r }
### clean up df for plot via function
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_plot(df = shr_1976_2022_state_gender,
                                          var = "victim_1_sex_clean") |> 
  ### arrange legend so that state name comes first followed by region name
  mutate(group_for_plot = fct_relevel(group_for_plot, paste0("Other ",csg_region," States"), after = Inf))


to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(victim_1_sex_clean, clearance_rate,
          group=group_for_plot),
    # color = jr_pal[1],
    # dashStyle = c("solid"),
    # opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by gender")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2018–2022") |>
  hc_caption(text = list("This analysis excludes records where a victim's gender is unknown<br>FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data")) |>     
# hc_add_theme(hc_theme_jc) |>
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()


```



### Age


```{r }

### clean up df for plot via function
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_plot(df = shr_1976_2022_state_age,
                                          var = "victim_1_age_bin") |> 
  ### arrange legend so that state name comes first followed by region name
  mutate(group_for_plot = fct_relevel(group_for_plot, paste0("Other ",csg_region," States"), after = Inf))


to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(victim_1_age_bin, clearance_rate,
          group = group_for_plot),
    # color = jr_pal[1],
    # dashStyle = c("solid"),
    # opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by age")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2018–2022") |>
  hc_caption(text = list("This analysis excludes records where a victim's age is unknown<br>FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data")) |> 
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()

```


### Weapon 

```{r }

### clean up df for plot via function
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_plot(df = shr_1976_2022_state_weapon,
                                          var = "offender_1_weapon_clean")  |> 
   filter(offender_1_weapon_clean%in%c("Firearm","Knife Or Cutting Instrument","Person Weapons - Includes Beating", "Blunt Object","Narcotics/Drugs")) |> 
  ### arrange legend so that state name comes first followed by region name
  mutate(group_for_plot = fct_relevel(group_for_plot, paste0("Other ",csg_region," States"), after = Inf))


to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(offender_1_weapon_clean, clearance_rate,
          group = group_for_plot),
    # color = jr_pal[1],
    # dashStyle = c("solid"),
    # opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by weapon")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2018–2022") |>
  hc_caption(text = list("This analysis includes the most common weapons across states in recent years<br>FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data")) |> 
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()


```


### Number of victims

```{r }

### clean up df for plot via function
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_plot(df = shr_1976_2022_state_single_multiple_victims,
                                          var = "victim_multiple_flag")  |>
  filter(!is.na(victim_multiple_flag)) |> 
  ### order by single victims and then multiple victims in viz
  mutate(victim_multiple_flag = factor(victim_multiple_flag,
    levels = c("Single Victim","Multiple Victims"))) |> 
  ### arrange legend so that state name comes first followed by region name
  mutate(group_for_plot = fct_relevel(group_for_plot, paste0("Other ",csg_region," States"), after = Inf))



to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(victim_multiple_flag, clearance_rate,
          group=group_for_plot),
    # color = jr_pal[1],
    # dashStyle = c("solid"),
    # opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by number of victims")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2018–2022") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data") |> 
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25),
      categories = levels(to_plot$victim_multiple_flag)
      # reversed = 'true',
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()


```

:::

<br>



::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse=true}

## Data sources and methodology

Data regarding crime and solve rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr){target="_parent"}. Counts of reported violent crime between 2013 and 2023 are derived from the Summary Reporting System (SRS) and the National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS). The FBI generated national estimates for reported crime and clearances based on agency-level reports, and these estimates were published through 2023 in the FBI's annual report, [_Crime in the U.S._](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019){target="_parent"}, and on the FBI's [Crime Data Explorer](https://cde.ucr.cjis.gov/LATEST/webapp/#/pages/home){target="_parent"}. For agency-level clearance data, we used [Jacob Kaplan's version of the Offenses Known and Clearances by Arrest](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/OESSD1) dataset.

The FBI stopped SRS data collection after data year 2020 and required law enforcement agencies to use NIBRS to report crime statistics to the FBI. This change decreased the number of agencies that submitted their crime data and thus reduced the reliability of state estimates in 2021. In 2022 and 2023, the FBI allowed agencies who were unable to submit NIBRS data to submit SRS data again. Ninety-four percent of the U.S. population was covered in the 2023 crime data submissions to the FBI, with most states providing close to complete data. However, the population upon which the estimates were derived decreased by over 10 percent in three states: Florida, Mississippi, and Wyoming, so conclusions about crime changes in those states should be interpreted with additional caution.

Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total U.S. population. Population data come from the U.S. Census Bureau [American Community Survey 1-year estimates (table B01003)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/data.html){target="_parent"} and the [2020 Decennial Census Redistricting Data (table P1)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/decennial-census/about/rdo/summary-files.html){target="_parent"}.

Solve rates are the number of reported crimes cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. A reported crime incident is [cleared by exceptional means](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019/topic-pages/clearances){target='_parent'} if a law enforcement agency is unable to arrest or formally charge a person with a crime, but the agency has met the following conditions:
    
a) Identified the person who committed the crime
b) Gathered enough evidence to support an arrest, make a charge, and turn over the person to the court for prosecution
c) Identified the suspect’s exact location so that the person could be taken into custody immediately
d) Encountered a circumstance outside the control of law enforcement that prohibits the agency from arresting, charging, and prosecuting the person
    
Examples of exceptional clearances include the death of the person who committed the crime; the victim’s refusal to cooperate with the prosecution after the person who committed the crime has been identified; or the denial of extradition.

In some instances, the solve rate and number of solved crimes may be less than 0. This is due to the fact that the number of reported crimes cleared in a year can include incidents reported in prior years. For this reason, it is possible to have more cleared crimes than new reported incidents in a given year.

Agency- and county-level violent crime and solve rate data presented above are restricted to agencies reporting data for all 12 months in 2023. The calculation of population covered by reporting agencies, as well as the number of reporting agencies by county, is also based on agencies with violent crime data for all 12 months. 

Each law enforcement agency is grouped by the county where they are based using the Law Enforcement Agency Identifiers Crosswalk developed by the Bureau of Justice Statistics in 2012. The overall county population used for calculation of county-level rates is based on the sum of the jurisdiction populations for agencies reporting data for all 12 months in 2023.

Counties with fewer than 25 reported violent incidents in 2023 are excluded from the map, as calculations of solve rates are not reliable. 

Data regarding state law enforcement expenditures were downloaded from the U.S. Census Bureau [Annual Survey of State and Local Government Finances](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/gov-finances.html){target="_parent"}.

Officer employment analysis is restricted to sworn law enforcement officers and was downloaded from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting (UCR) Program police employee data files](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019/topic-pages/police-employee-data){target="_parent"}.

Data on homicide solve rate trends and case characteristics come from the FBI's [Supplementary Homicide Reporting (SHR) Program](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019/topic-pages/expanded-homicide){target="_parent"}.

:::
